title: Add a playable game experience to the app

requirements.text: Add a playable in-browser game to the frontend (single-player) with clear rules, win/lose conditions, and keyboard + on-screen controls suitable for desktop and mobile.
acceptanceCriteria: A user can start a new game from the UI without reloading the page.
The game supports keyboard controls on desktop and touch controls on mobile.
The game includes a visible score and an end-of-game state with a restart option.
All user-facing text is in English.

requirements.text: Implement a persistent high-score system tied to the signed-in Internet Identity principal, including saving a user’s best score and showing a global leaderboard.
acceptanceCriteria: If the user is signed in, finishing a game can submit the score to the backend.
The backend stores and returns each principal’s best score (only updates when the new score is higher).
The frontend can display the current user’s best score and a global top leaderboard list.
If the user is not signed in, the UI clearly indicates that saving scores requires sign-in (English text).

requirements.text: Create and apply a coherent visual theme for the game UI (colors, typography, layout, and component styling) and use it consistently across the game screens (menu, gameplay HUD, game over, leaderboard). Avoid blue/purple as primary theme colors.
acceptanceCriteria: Game-related screens share a consistent visual style (background, typography, button styles, spacing).
The primary palette does not rely on blue/purple as the main theme colors.
UI remains readable and usable on small screens.

constraints: Backend must remain a single Motoko actor in backend/main.mo; only add migration.mo if upgrading existing stable state is required.
Do not edit frontend paths marked immutable by the system (compose existing UI components instead).
Do not use third-party authentication providers; only Internet Identity.
No WebSockets or real-time multiplayer features.
nonGoals: External databases or third-party backend services.
